bottomPanel.selectPodFirst: Select a terminal to view details

createPod.title: Create Worker
createPod.noOnlineRunnersHint: No online clusters in this organization. Switch to an org with a registered cluster, or register one under Infra → Clusters.
createPod.runnerHostHint: Clusters provide compute. Workers are isolated workspaces started from the selected image.
createPod.imageHint: Workers start from an image such as Claude Code, Codex, or Gemini.
createPod.noImagesForCluster: No images available on this cluster
createPod.modelResourceHint: Credentials are resolved from the selected AI resource at launch time.
createPod.noModelResourcesAvailableHint: No compatible model resources are available. Add one in Settings → AI Resources.
createPod.noRuntimeBundlesAvailableHint: This image has no runtime env bundles yet. Add one in Settings → Images.
createPod.noRuntimeBundleSelectedHint: No runtime bundle selected; the Worker runs without any extra runtime preferences.
createPod.repositoryOptionalHint: Optional. Link a Git repository to clone into the Worker workspace.
createPod.repositoryLinkedHint: The selected repository will be cloned when the Worker starts.
createPod.emptyRepositoriesDescription: Import a Git repository to clone it into the Worker sandbox.
createPod.promptPlaceholder: Enter the initial task for the Worker…
createPod.aliasPlaceholder: Enter a display name for this Worker…
createPod.perpetualDescription: Auto-restart when the process exits. Worker stays alive indefinitely.

automationLevel.label: Automation Level
automationLevel.interactiveHint: Approve every step. You stay in the loop for each action.
automationLevel.auto_editHint: Auto-approve reads and edits; only risky actions ask for confirmation.
automationLevel.autonomousHint: Fully autonomous, non-interactive execution. Runs in ACP mode.

createPod.noBundleHint: No runtime environment variable bundle is selected.
createPod.multiBundleHint: Selected bundles are merged into the Pod env in order; later entries override earlier ones on conflicting keys.
createPod.noSkillsAvailableHint: No skills installed for this repository. Install from Settings → Extensions or the Skills sidebar.
createPod.noSkillsSelectedHint: No skills selected; all installed skills compatible with this agent will be loaded.
createPod.skillsSelectedHint: Selected skills are attached to the Pod via the SKILLS AgentFile directive.
createPod.skillsRequireRepositoryHint: Select a repository to load skills installed on that repo.
createPod.runtimeConfigDescription: Configure Worker lifetime, mount knowledge bases, and load skills from a repository.
createPod.stepClusterImageDescription: Pick the execution cluster and Worker image (AI runtime).
createPod.stepRuntimePanelHint: Configure cluster, image, model resource, Git repository, interaction mode, duration, and initial task.
createPod.credentialModelDescription: Select the exact AI resource this Worker will use. Runtime env bundles remain optional and never carry model credentials.
createPod.stepCapabilitiesPanelHint: Configure knowledge base and skill mounts separately.
createPod.knowledgeConfigDescription: Select knowledge bases to clone into the Worker sandbox; toggle read-only or read-write per mount.
createPod.skillConfigDescription: Select installed skills from the linked repository to attach to the agent.
createPod.modelBindingDescription: Bind this Worker to a virtual API key so its token usage is attributed to that key's quota.
createPod.modelBindingHint: Leave unbound when usage should be attributed only to the selected AI resource.
createPod.budgetConfigDescription: Optionally cap this Worker's token budget. Organization quota still applies and is enforced at creation.
createPod.budgetOrgUsageHint: Read-only. Pod creation is blocked once the organization quota is exceeded.
createPod.budgetCapHint: Leave empty for no per-Worker cap. When set, it is passed to the agent as token_budget.
createPod.stepCapabilitiesDescription: Mount knowledge bases and skills from the linked repository.
createPod.stepAgentInstructionsDescription: Preview the generated AgentFile layer, or switch to custom AgentFile / AGENTS.md.
createPod.stepTaskDescription: Initial task, interaction mode, and short- vs long-term usage.
createPod.selectImageFirst: Select an image first to configure this section.
createPod.skillsSectionHint: Skills load from the selected repository; leave empty to use the image defaults.
createPod.agentInstructionsDescription: Form choices above are merged into AgentFile; override PROMPT, SKILLS, KNOWLEDGE, etc. manually.
createPod.agentInstructionsEmptyPreview: (Nothing generated yet — configure knowledge, skills, or the initial task to preview here.)
createPod.agentInstructionsFooter: In custom mode, the editor text is submitted as-is instead of merging form fields.
createPod.agentInstructionsSourceHint: Switch to Source mode (top right) to edit this AgentFile directly.
createPod.sourceModeHint: Edit the AgentFile layer directly. Cluster, image, and automation level stay as form fields; everything else (env vars, prompt, repo, skills) is written as AgentFile directives.
createPod.customEnvDescription: Inject custom environment variables into the Worker process. Emitted as ENV directives in the AgentFile layer.
createPod.customEnvPlaintextWarning: Values are stored in plaintext in the AgentFile layer. Do not put secrets here; use AI Resources for model credentials.
createPod.durationShortDescription: Resources are reclaimed after the task finishes — good for one-off work and debugging.
createPod.durationLongDescription: Auto-restarts when the process exits — good for ongoing development and session work.
createPod.noKnowledgeBasesHint: No knowledge bases yet. Create one, then mount it when starting a Worker.
createPod.knowledgeBasesHint: Knowledge bases are cloned into the Worker sandbox under kb/; rw mounts allow the agent to commit and push changes.
createPod.lifecycleDescription: Fine-grained control over when the Worker is destroyed (overrides the duration preset above).
createPod.lifecycleManualHint: No automatic destroy time when using manual destroy.

lifecyclePolicy.manualDesc: Worker stays accessible until you terminate it.
lifecyclePolicy.idleDesc: Good for temporary debugging and short tasks.
lifecyclePolicy.completedDesc: Good for one-shot tasks; cleanup runs after the agent finishes.

skills.description: Browse and install skills for your agents
skills.hint: Install skills to a repository to use them in pods

1.title: Choose runtime
1.description: Bind an exact model, Worker type, immutable image, compute target, deployment mode, and resource profile.

2.title: Configure Worker type
2.description: Set only fields declared by the selected Worker type. Secrets remain references to credential bundles.

3.title: Configure workspace
3.description: Attach a repository, capabilities, instructions, task, interaction mode, and lifecycle.

4.title: Review and create
4.description: Server preflight resolves every scoped reference before the immutable WorkerSpec snapshot is created.

typeChange.title: Change Worker type?
typeChange.description: Type-specific model, image, configuration, Skill, and environment selections will be cleared.

typeConfig.optionsRequired: Worker creation options must load before type configuration.
typeConfig.versionMismatch: Worker type schema version does not match the selected option.
typeConfig.empty: This Worker type has no additional configuration fields.
typeConfig.invalid: Worker type configuration schema is invalid.
typeConfig.secretHint: Secrets are referenced from a credential bundle and are never stored in the Worker draft.

workspace.branchRequired: Branch is required when a repository is selected.
workspace.missingReferences: One or more selected Skill or environment references are no longer available. Select them again before preflight.

preflight.missingResolvedSpec: Preflight did not return a resolved WorkerSpec. Run preflight again.
